name: Linde eternal (chaotic) inflation
summary: Quantum fluctuations of the inflaton during chaotic inflation locally drive the inflaton field upward in some regions, where slow-roll inflation continues forever even as inflation ends elsewhere. Produces a fractal multiverse of bubble universes with different effective low-energy physics; provides the natural cosmological setting for the anthropic-landscape interpretation of the cosmological-constant problem.

addresses_puzzles.note: Once inflation starts somewhere, eternal self-reproduction guarantees infinite inflating regions, making the 'why did inflation start' question less acute. The 'measure problem' (how to compute probabilities in an infinite multiverse) remains an active issue.

Combined with a string-theory landscape, eternal inflation populates all metastable vacua, making anthropic selection (Weinberg's bound) operationally meaningful. Anthropic landscape requires eternal inflation to make sense as a probabilistic explanation.

introduces.note: Builds on inflationary mechanism. New 'parameter' is the choice of measure for computing observer-weighted predictions in the infinite multiverse.

history: Linde (1986), 'Eternally existing self-reproducing chaotic inflationary universe', Phys. Lett. B 175, 395 — formalised stochastic eternal inflation in the chaotic inflation context. Vilenkin (1983) had earlier proposed eternal inflation in the new-inflation framework. Underlies most modern multiverse discussions.

references.citation: Linde, 'Eternally existing self-reproducing chaotic inflationary universe', Phys. Lett. B 175, 395 (1986)
